string.text: The Browning Automatic Rifle (BAR) is an iconic selective-fire rifle renowned for its versatility and firepower. Developed in the early 20th century, this gas-operated, air-cooled firearm gained fame for its service during World War I and II. Weighing around 16 pounds, the BAR fired the powerful .30-06 Springfield cartridge, offering a substantial rate of fire, typically between 500 to 650 rounds per minute. Its portability, reliability, and adaptability in various combat scenarios, from infantry support to mobile firepower, solidified its status as a classic light machine gun in military history.\n\n
Magazine for the BAR Assault Rifle, houses 20 rounds of .308 NATO (7.62x51) ammunition. \n \n
